name: kubectl
description: Kubernetes command-line tool for managing clusters, deploying apps, and inspecting resources.

commands.name: apply
commands.description: Apply a configuration from a YAML/JSON file

commands.name: logs
commands.description: Stream logs from a running pod

commands.name: exec
commands.description: Execute a command inside a running container

use_cases: Check pod status and health in a cluster
Deploy or update applications via manifests
Debug failing containers by reading logs or exec-ing in
agent_metadata.example_workflow: kubectl get pods -n production --no-headers | grep -v Running && kubectl logs <failing-pod> --tail=50
